meaning: n.扭,拧vt.夺取,猛扭,歪曲,曲解,费力取得
examples: To wriggle is to wrest about like a worm.
I will say thou didst break in and wrest the prisoner from my hands by force.
For centuries he braved arctic cold, tropic heat and inhuman privations to wrest gold from the earth.
For its part, AIG is suing to wrest back the chunk of stock held by C.
Opposition parties are hoping to seize on Musharraf's rising unpopularity to wrest control of the parliament.
